statement: The source-inactive constants theorem supplies the normalized finite inactive-tail constant and proves it is absorbable by the finite low/mid Schur budget.

finiteInactiveTailBoundStatus.label: finite certified inactive-tail bound
finiteInactiveTailBoundStatus.reason: Imported from the source-inactive constants theorem: the normalized source model has the certified inactive-tail constant epsilon_delta.

lowMidAbsorptionBudgetStatus.label: low/mid Schur absorption budget
lowMidAbsorptionBudgetStatus.reason: Imported from the source-inactive constants theorem: epsilon_delta is below the finite low/mid Schur budget.

tailBoundConsequenceStatus.label: source-inactive tail bound consequence
tailBoundConsequenceStatus.reason: The finite inactive-tail constant and its absorption budget are both closed.

proof: Import the normalized finite inactive-tail constant.
Import the low/mid Schur absorption comparison.
Forward the epsilon, budget, and slack values needed by high-block min-max assembly.
